blog.cta_text: Get weekly money tips to protect and grow your wealth

scenes.voiceover_script: Every year, people lose billions to investment scams. The pitch sounds perfect: guaranteed returns, exclusive access, limited time offer. But here's the truth: most scams share the same missing ingredients. And once you know what to look for, you'll never fall for them again.
scenes.h2_heading: Why Smart People Still Fall for Scams

scenes.voiceover_script: Every legitimate investment passes a simple 4-part test. First, licensing: is it regulated by authorities like the SEC? Second, credibility: does it have real assets or track record? Third, production: does it generate actual value? Fourth, obligation: what duties does the provider owe you? If any part fails, walk away.

Scammers are masters at faking one or two elements. They'll show fake licenses, fabricate returns, or promise obligations they never intend to keep. But passing all four parts? That's nearly impossible to fake. The more boxes left unchecked, the bigger the red flag.
scenes.h2_heading: Why This Test Works Against Fraudsters

scenes.voiceover_script: This applies to you if you've ever received a hot stock tip, been pitched a crypto opportunity, or considered an investment with unusually high returns. If something promises huge gains with zero risk, your scam radar should be going off.

Before investing anything, ask these four questions: Is this registered with regulators? Can I verify the track record independently? Does this create real economic value? What legal protections do I have? Write down the answers. If you can't answer all four confidently, keep your money in your pocket.
Want more tools to protect your money? Follow MoneyWise for weekly tips on building wealth safely. Because the best return is keeping what you've already earned.
